name: DropdownFilters
description: Read-only 'active filters' mini-panel adjacent to the main DropdownOptions. Mirrors the FilterChips pinned to the TableTopBar filter row — clicking a chip jumps focus to the matching top-bar chip and opens its value-side dropdown. Filter addition is NOT supported from this panel (use Filters.addMenu instead).

props.name: children
props.description: Custom filter chip content. When provided, options is ignored. Pass FilterChip instances directly for stateful / interactive use.

props.name: options
props.description: Convenience: simple filter options ({ id, label }[]) rendered as size-S FilterChip with 'All' as the value summary. Useful for static demos.

props.name: onSelect
props.description: Called when a simple option chip's chevron is clicked (only used by the options code path).

states.rationale: The panel surface itself is a passive floating container — no panel-level hover/pressed/focus. The empty state is purely conditional rendering. Interactive states live on the composed FilterChip children (chip-level overlay hover, focus ring on the value-side chevron Button) and inherit from List + ListItem styling.

keyboard.Tab: Move focus to the next FilterChip (label part / value-side chevron) inside the panel
keyboard.Shift+Tab: Move focus to the previous focusable element
keyboard.Enter: On a focused FilterChip label: toggle visibility (aria-pressed). On a focused value-side chevron: open the chip's value dropdown.
keyboard.Space: Same as Enter for native buttons
keyboard.Escape: Closes any open chip value-dropdown (handled by FilterChip itself)

accessibility.focusManagement: Root is role="dialog" with aria-label="Filters". The panel does not own focus management beyond rendering — each composed FilterChip carries its own keyboard / focus contract (Space/Enter on the label toggles visibility; chevron opens the value popover). The cross-surface focus-handoff (click a chip here → jump to and open the matching top-bar chip) is the consumer's responsibility.

propertyMapping.note: Derived in code — there is no empty prop on the React component. Whether children or options were provided drives which path renders.
Custom FilterChip instances. When provided, the options shortcut is ignored. Use for stateful chips with their own value lists.

useWhen: Adjacent to the main DropdownOptions panel, opened from its 'Filters' row
Quick way to inspect / jump to active filters when the top-bar filter row is not currently visible
doNotUseFor: Adding a new filter → use the Plus button at the end of the Filters row (driven by Filters.addMenu)
Selection / multi-choice value lists → use Dropdown / DropdownAutocomplete

knownDeviations.description: Spec mentions --dls-color-component-dropdown-filters-* tokens but they do not exist in tokens.json. CSS uses semantic surface/border tokens directly via the composed <List>.
The Figma empty boolean is derived in code from whether children/options were provided. There is no empty prop on the React component.
